title: Beginning Theory: An Introduction to Literary and Cultural Theory
excerpt: The most widely used introductory textbook to literary and cultural theory, offering clear, balanced coverage of every major theoretical school from structuralism to ecocriticism.
keyIdeas: Theory is a toolkit, not a dogma — each approach offers different tools for different reading tasks
The distinction between 'theory before theory' and 'theory after theory' provides a navigable map of the field
Every theoretical position has both strengths and weaknesses; none is universally applicable
Practical criticism and theoretical reading are complementary, not opposed
keyTakeaways: Understand the major schools of literary theory and their key concepts
Recognise the historical development of theory from the 1960s to the present
Apply theoretical approaches to literary texts with confidence
whoShouldRead: Undergraduate students encountering literary theory for the first time
General readers who want a clear, non-intimidating guide to theory
Teachers looking for a reliable classroom textbook
whoShouldSkip: Advanced scholars who already know the field well
Readers seeking in-depth coverage of a single theorist or school
